question text: What is the best scientific definition of "buoyancy"?
a: The upward force exerted by a fluid (like water) that opposes the weight of an object.
b: The magnetic attraction between water molecules and plastic.
c: The downward pull of the Earth on an object.
d: The friction caused by water moving against a smooth surface.
e: The speed at which an object sinks to the bottom.
f: The weight of the air inside a container.

question text: Which description best explains the force of "gravity" acting on our submarine?
a: A force that pushes objects sideways through the water.
b: A force that pushes objects up toward the sky.
c: A force that pulls objects down toward the center of the Earth.
d: A force that only exists when the bottle is empty.
e: A force created by the syringe pushing air.
f: A force that stops the water from entering the bottle.

question text: In science, what is the simplest definition of a "force"?
b: The amount of matter in an object.
d: A measure of how hot something is.

question text: Using the syringe and tube connected to the bottle cap, what action makes the submarine RISE to the surface?
b: Pulling the plunger back to suck air out.
d: Filling the bottle completely with sand.
e: Disconnecting the syringe from the tube.
f: Pushing the plunger down to force air into the bottle, pushing the water out.

question text: Using the syringe and tube, what action makes the submarine SINK to the bottom?
a: Adding more styrofoam to the outside.
b: Pulling the plunger back to remove air, allowing water to enter the bottle.
c: Pushing the plunger to add more air.
e: Heating up the water in the tank.
f: Squeezing the plastic bottle with your hand.

question text: Why does pushing air into the bottle make the submarine float?
a: Air is much less dense (lighter) than the water it pushes out.
b: Air is heavier than water, so it pushes off the bottom.
c: The air dissolves the plastic bottle.
d: The air creates a magnetic field.
e: The air creates suction that pulls the bottle up.
f: The air turns into steam and lifts the bottle like a rocket.

question text: When the submarine is floating perfectly still on the surface of the water, which statement about forces is true?
a: There are no forces acting on the bottle.
b: Gravity is much stronger than Buoyancy.
c: Friction is the only force acting on the bottle.
d: The downward force of Gravity is balanced (equal) to the upward force of Buoyancy.
f: The force of the water is pushing the bottle down.

question text: When the submarine is sinking towards the bottom, which force is stronger?
c: The magnetic force is stronger than Gravity.
e: The tension in the tube is lifting the bottle.
f: The force of the air is stronger than the water.

question text: Why do we tape weights (like metal nuts or washers) to the bottom of the plastic bottle submarine?
a: To make the bottle look shiny.
b: To block the holes so water cannot get in.
c: To make the bottle float higher in the water.
e: To add mass so the bottle stays upright and is heavy enough to sink when water enters.
f: To protect the plastic from scratching.

question text: Why are there holes drilled in the bottom of the plastic bottle submarine?
a: To make the bottle more aerodynamic.
b: To let the air escape bubbles out the top.
c: To allow water to flow in and out as we change the air pressure.
d: To let us see inside the bottle.
e: To decrease the friction of the water.
f: To allow fish to swim inside.
